text: Many actions are included with flow designer, what are some frequently used core actions? (Choose four.)
explanation: Create Record, Ask for Approval, Look Up Record, and Wait for Condition are real Flow Designer core actions; 'Look for Update' and 'Wait for Match' are not standard action names.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Look for Update' and 'Wait for Match' sound plausible but aren't real action names — don't confuse them with the actual 'Update Record' and 'Wait for Condition' actions.

text: Which group of permissions is used to control Application and Module access?
explanation: Roles control which applications and modules a user can see/access; ACLs control record/field-level data access.
wrongAnswerNotes: Access Control Rules is the tempting pick since it sounds like a permission mechanism, but ACLs govern data access, not menu/module visibility.

text: Which one of the following is NOT a type of Visual Task Board?
explanation: Visual Task Board types are Freeform, Flexible, and Guided. 'Feature' is an Agile artifact, not a board type.
wrongAnswerNotes: Freeform, Flexible, and Guided are all genuine VTB types — don't second-guess yourself into picking one of those; only 'Feature' isn't a board type.

text: Access Control rules may be defined with which of the following permission requirements? (Choose three.)
explanation: ACL permission requirements are built from Roles, Conditional Expressions, and Scripts.
wrongAnswerNotes: User Criteria is a trap — it's used for Service Catalog/Knowledge visibility, not as an ACL requirement type. ACLs use Roles, Conditions, and Scripts only.

text: Which collaboration tool opens a sidebar and allows you to create new conversations with other ServiceNow users?
explanation: Connect Chat opens a sidebar panel for starting conversations with other ServiceNow users.
wrongAnswerNotes: Agent Chat is a different feature for live customer support conversations, not the general sidebar messenger — don't mix them up.

text: When looking at a long list of records, you want to quickly filter, to show only those which have Category of Hardware. How might you do that?

options.A: On the Category column header, right click and select Show > Hardware
options.B: Right click on magnifier, type Hardware and click enter
options.C: On the list, locate and right click on the value Hardware, select Show Matching
options.D: On Breadcrumb, click > icon, type Hardware and click enter
options.E: Click Funnel icon, type Hardware and click enter

explanation: Right-clicking a value in a list and choosing 'Show Matching' quickly filters to that value.
wrongAnswerNotes: The column-header option sounds plausible but that submenu path doesn't exist — the real gesture is right-clicking the VALUE itself in the list, not the column header.

text: A REQ number in the Service Catalog represents

options.D: the individual item in the order.

explanation: A REQ (Request) number represents the overall order number in the Service Catalog.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'The individual item in the order' describes an RITM (Requested Item), not the REQ — don't mix up the overall request number with the line-item number.

text: A user wants to create a set of filter conditions, where they want to show records which satisfy two conditions: ✑ Incidents where the state is Closed ✑ Incidents where Assignment Group is Network After clicking the Funnel icon, what should the user do?

options.A: Define the first condition; click AND button; define second condition; click Run
options.B: Define the first condition; click AND button; define second condition; press enter
options.C: Define the first condition; click OR button; define second condition; press enter
options.D: Define the first condition; click > icon on breadcrumb, define second condition; click Run
options.E: Define the first condition; click > icon on breadcrumb, define second condition; press enter

explanation: After defining two conditions joined by AND in the condition builder, you click Run to execute the filter.
wrongAnswerNotes: Pressing Enter works for a single condition, but once you've added an AND clause you need to click Run to execute the combined filter.

text: What do you activate when you want to add applications or functionality within your development instance?
explanation: Plugins are activated to add new applications/functionality to an instance.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'App Package' isn't a real ServiceNow term — the actual mechanism is activating a Plugin.

text: What is (are) best practice(s) regarding users/groups/roles? (Choose two.)

options.A: You should never assign roles to groups.
options.B: You should assign roles to users.
options.C: You should add users to groups.
options.D: You should assign roles to groups.

explanation: Best practice: add users to groups, and assign roles to groups (not directly to individual users).
wrongAnswerNotes: Assigning roles directly to users works technically but is against best practice — always assign roles to groups and add users to the group instead.

text: When looking at a long list of records, you want to quickly filter, to show only those which have Short Description containing email. How might you do that?

options.A: Click List Magnifier to expand column search, on Short Description, type email, click enter
options.B: On Search box, select text, type email, click enter
options.C: Click List Magnifier to expand column search, on Short Description, type *email, click enter
options.D: Click List Magnifier to expand column search, on Short Description, type %email, click enter

explanation: Using the list column (Magnifier) search on Short Description with 'email' performs a contains-search by default.
wrongAnswerNotes: No wildcard is needed — the column search box already performs a contains-match by default when you just type 'email'.

text: From a form, what would you click to add additional fields to the form? (Choose two.)

options.D: Right click on header > Add > Field
options.F: Right click on header > Configure > UX Dashboard

explanation: Configure > Form Layout adds existing fields to a form; Configure > Form Design lets you create/add new fields visually.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Context Menu > Form > Layout' is a near-miss on the real menu path — it's Configure > Form Layout, not Form > Layout.

text: What field contains a record's 32-character, unique identifier?
explanation: sys_id is the 32-character unique identifier on every record.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'id' sounds generic enough to be right, but ServiceNow's actual unique identifier field is specifically named sys_id.

text: Which would NOT appear in the History section of the Application Navigator?
explanation: History tracks recently viewed Records, Lists, and Forms; UI Pages are not tracked there.
wrongAnswerNotes: History legitimately tracks Records, Lists, and Forms — UI Pages are the only one excluded.

text: A customer requests the following data quality measures be added: • Incident numbers should be read only, on all lists and forms, for all users. • Short Description field should be mandatory, on all records, across all applications, on Insert. Which type of policy would you use to meet this requirement?
explanation: A Data Policy enforces mandatory/read-only rules regardless of how data enters the system (UI, import, web service).
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Data Quality Policy' is a trap built right into the question's own wording — the actual ServiceNow feature is simply called a Data Policy.

text: The display sequence is controlled in a Service Catalog Item using which of the following?

options.A: The Default Value field in the Catalog Item form
options.B: The Sequence field in the Catalog Item form
options.C: The Order field in the Variable form
options.D: The Choice field in the Variable form

explanation: The Order field on the Variable form controls the display sequence of variables/questions on a catalog item.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Sequence field' sounds right by name, but display order is controlled by the Order field on each Variable, not a Catalog Item-level field.

text: On the knowledge base record, which tab would you use to define which users are not able to write articles to the knowledge base?
explanation: The 'Cannot Contribute' tab on a knowledge base record defines users who cannot author/write articles.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Cannot Author' is a near-identical sounding decoy — the real tab name is specifically 'Cannot Contribute'.

text: Which of the following concepts are associated with the ServiceNow CMDB? (Choose four.)
explanation: CMDB concepts: it's a database, made of tables/fields, supports service processes, and the Dependency View.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'User Permissions' sounds CMDB-adjacent but isn't a core CMDB concept — the real four are Service Processes, Tables/Fields, the Database itself, and Dependency View.

text: Which of the following statements is true when a new table is created by extending another table?

options.A: The new table archives the parent table and assumed its roles in the database
options.B: The new table inherits all of the Business Rules, Client Scripts, and UI Policies of the parent table, but none of the existing fields
options.C: The new table inherits all of the fields of the parent table and can also contain new fields unique to itself
options.D: The new table inherits all of the fields, but does not inherit Access Control rules, Client Scripts, and UI Policies of the parent table

explanation: An extended table inherits all parent fields and can add its own unique fields.
wrongAnswerNotes: The option about inheriting rules but not fields sounds authoritative but is backwards — extended tables inherit ACLs, Client Scripts, and UI Policies along with fields, not just fields alone.

text: Tables may be set up with Many to Many relationships. What is a classic example of a scenario where the tables would have many to many relationships?

options.A: Vendors can sell multiple products; and products can be sold by multiple vendors.
options.B: A Task can trigger many Workflows; and a Workflow can trigger many Tasks.
options.C: Requests can contain many Items; and Items can be any item from the catalog.
options.D: A Configuration Item can belong to multiple Classes, and Classes can contain multiple Configuration Items.

explanation: Vendors selling multiple products (and vice versa) is the classic many-to-many example.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'CI can belong to multiple Classes' option describes a real relationship but isn't the textbook example ServiceNow calls out — Vendors/Products is the standard many-to-many illustration.

text: What is the primary application used to load data into ServiceNow?
explanation: System Import Sets is the primary application used to load data into ServiceNow.
wrongAnswerNotes: Don't confuse System Update Sets with System Import Sets — Update Sets move customizations, Import Sets move data.

text: What is a formatter? Select one of the following.

options.A: A formatter allows you to configure applications on your instance
options.B: A formatter is a form element used to display information that is not a field in the record
options.C: A formatter allows you to populate fields automatically
options.D: A formatter is a set of conditions applied to a table to help find and work with data

explanation: A formatter is a form element that displays information that isn't a table field (e.g. attachments, activity stream).
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Populate fields automatically' describes an auto-population mechanism (like a default value), not a formatter — a formatter specifically displays non-field information on a form.

text: Knowledge Base Search results can be sorted by which of the following? (Choose three.)
explanation: Knowledge search results can be sorted by most recent update, relevancy, and number of views.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Popularity' overlaps with 'Number of views' as a decoy — the three real sort options are Most recent update, Relevancy, and Number of views.

text: Which one of the following describes the primary operations performed against tables in the ServiceNow platform?
explanation: ACL/table operations in ServiceNow are Create, Read, Write, Delete (CRUD).
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Create, Read, Upload, Delete' is a near-identical decoy that swaps 'Write' for 'Upload' — the real ACL/table operations are Create, Read, Write, Delete.

text: How do you make a list filter available to everyone?

options.A: Make active, assign a name, and save
options.B: Assign a group, set visibility, and save
options.C: Assign a name, set visibility, and save
options.D: Make active, set visibility, and save

explanation: To make a list filter public, assign it a name, set its visibility, and save.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Make active, set visibility, and save' looks right but skips naming the filter — you also need to assign it a name for others to identify it.

text: What is a quick way to create a report from a list view?

options.A: Click on filter breadcrumb, drag and drop on the Report > Create New module
options.B: Click Funnel, define filter conditions, click Create Report
options.C: Click Context Menu, select Create Report
options.D: Apply filter, right click on column header, select Bar Chart
options.E: Apply filter, right click on column header, select Create Report

explanation: The List context menu includes a 'Create Report' option that carries over the current filter.
wrongAnswerNotes: The column-header report option sounds equally valid, but the documented quick path is via the List Context Menu, not a column header submenu.

text: An IT manager is responsible for the Network and Hardware assignment groups, each group contains 5 team members. These team members are working on many tasks, but the manager cannot see any tasks on the Service Desk > My Groups Work list. What could explain this?

options.A: The Service Desk > My Groups Work list shows active work tasks that are not yet assigned.
options.B: The manager does not have the itil role.
options.C: The manager is not a member of the Service Desk group.
options.D: The manager is not a member of the Network and Hardware groups.
options.E: The Assignment Group manager field is empty.

explanation: My Groups Work only shows tasks for groups the logged-in user is a member of; the manager isn't a member of those groups.
wrongAnswerNotes: The itil-role option is a tempting red herring — the actual cause is group membership: My Groups Work only shows groups the logged-in user personally belongs to.

text: Which of the following can be customized through the Basic Configuration UI 16 module? (Choose three.)
explanation: Basic Configuration UI16 manages branding items like Banner Image and Browser Tab Title.
wrongAnswerNotes: System Date Format and Form Header Size sound plausible for a 'basic configuration' screen, but they're managed elsewhere in System Properties, not Basic Configuration UI16. (Flagged low-confidence — worth double-checking against the docs.)

text: When importing spreadsheet data into ServiceNow, in which step does the data get written to the receiving table?
explanation: Running the Transform (Run Transform) is the step where data is actually written to the target table.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Execute Transform' is a wording trap — the actual step/button is named Run Transform.

text: You have heard about a new application released by ServiceNow. You want to try it out, to see if it might be useful for your company's ServiceNow implementation. What would be the best way to get hands-on experience with the new application?

options.A: Search the wiki for the sales demo request form
options.B: Check the latest release notes at docs servicenow.com
options.C: Activate the application plug in, on your personal dev instance
options.D: Activate the application plug in, on your company's production instance.

explanation: Activating the plugin on a personal dev instance is the safe way to try a new application hands-on.
wrongAnswerNotes: Activating on the production instance is risky and wrong — always trial new plugins on a personal dev instance first, never production.

text: Which collaboration tool is available from the banner, using the bubble icon?
explanation: Connect Chat is opened from the banner's chat bubble icon.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Now Messenger' isn't the actual product name for this feature — it's Connect Chat, accessed via the banner bubble icon.

text: What is specified in an Access Control rule?

options.B: Table Schema, CRUD, and User Authentication
options.C: Object and Operation being secured; Permissions required to access the object

explanation: An ACL specifies the object/operation being secured and the permissions (roles/conditions/scripts) required.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Table Schema, CRUD, User Authentication' mixes in real-sounding concepts, but an ACL specifically defines the object/operation plus required permissions — not a schema or full auth flow.

text: Which three Variable Types can be added to a Service Catalog Item?

options.C: Number List, Single Line Text, and Reference
options.D: Multiple Choice, Select Box, and Checkbox

explanation: Multiple Choice, Select Box, and Checkbox are all valid Service Catalog variable types.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Ordered' isn't a real variable type — watch for invented-sounding options mixed with genuine ones.

text: What is the function of user impersonation?
explanation: Impersonation is used for testing and visibility into what a specific user can see/do.
wrongAnswerNotes: The other options invent plausible-sounding technical features (verbose logging, custom perspectives) that don't actually describe impersonation's purpose.

text: A Service Catalog may include which of the following components?

options.B: Order Guides, Catalog Items, and Interceptors
options.C: Catalog Items, Asset Contracts, Task Surveys
options.D: Record Producers, Order Guides, and Catalog Items

explanation: A Service Catalog can include Record Producers, Order Guides, and Catalog Items.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Interceptors' isn't a genuine Service Catalog component — don't be fooled by technical-sounding invented terms.

text: What are the steps for applying an update set to an instance?
explanation: Applying an Update Set follows Retrieve, Preview, Commit.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Retrieve, Assess, Apply' is a near-miss on the real sequence — it's Preview and Commit, not Assess and Apply.

text: Which one of the following statements describes the contents of the Configuration Management Database (CMDB)?

options.A: The CMDB contains data about tangible and intangible business assets
options.B: The CMDB contains the Business Rules that direct the intangible, configurable assets used by a company
options.C: The CMDB archives all Service Management PaaS equipment metadata and usage statistics
options.D: The CMDB contains ITIL process data pertaining to configuration items

explanation: The CMDB holds data about tangible and intangible business assets (configuration items).
wrongAnswerNotes: 'ITIL process data' sounds ITSM-relevant but the CMDB itself holds configuration item/asset data, not ITIL process definitions.

text: Which type of scripts run in the browser?
explanation: UI Policies and Client Scripts execute in the browser (client-side).
wrongAnswerNotes: Business Rules run server-side, not in the browser — that's the classic Client Script vs Business Rule confusion.

text: Reports can be created from which different places in the platform? (Choose two.)
explanation: Reports can be created from a list's column heading context menu, or from the Reports > View/Run module.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Statistics module' sounds reporting-adjacent but isn't a real report-creation entry point — reports start from a list's column heading or Reports > View/Run.

text: Which one statement correctly describes Access Control rule evaluation?

options.A: Table access rules are evaluated from the general to the specific
options.B: If more than one rule applies to a record, the older rule is evaluated first
options.C: If a row level rule and a field level rule exist, both rules must be true before an operation is allowed
options.D: The role with the most permissions evaluates the rules first.

explanation: Both a row-level (table) rule and a field-level rule must evaluate true before an operation is allowed.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Evaluated general to specific' reverses the real rule — ACLs are evaluated specific to general, not the other way around.

text: Which of the following is used to initiate a flow?
explanation: A Trigger is what initiates (starts) a flow.
wrongAnswerNotes: An Event can be part of automation but the component that specifically initiates a flow is its Trigger.

text: Table Access Control rules are processed in the following order:

options.A: any table name (wildcard), parent table name, table name
options.B: table name, parent table name, any table name (wildcard)
options.C: parent table name, table name, any table name (wildcard)
options.D: any table name (wildcard), table name, parent table name

explanation: Table ACLs are processed: specific table name, then parent table name, then wildcard (*).
wrongAnswerNotes: The wildcard-first option reverses the real evaluation order — it's specific table first, wildcard last, not the other way around.

text: Which tool is used to have conversations with logged-in users in real-time?
explanation: Connect Chat provides real-time conversations with logged-in users.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Now Messenger' is again a plausible-sounding but incorrect name — the feature is Connect Chat.

text: When using the Data Pill Picker, use which keys to dot-walk to fields in other tables?
explanation: Arrow keys are used in the Data Pill Picker to dot-walk into referenced fields on other tables.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Ctrl <, Ctrl >' looks like a plausible keyboard shortcut, but the actual dot-walk navigation in the Data Pill Picker uses simple Arrow keys.

text: The ServiceNow platform includes which types of interfaces? (Choose three.)
explanation: The platform's interface types include Now Mobile Apps, Service Portals, and Now Platform User Interfaces.
wrongAnswerNotes: Agent Control Center is a real ServiceNow feature but it's a specific telephony/CSM tool, not one of the platform's general interface categories.

text: Which one of the following statements is true about Column Context Menus?

options.A: It displays actions such as creating quick reports, configuring the list, and exporting data
options.B: It displays actions related to filtering options, assigning tags, and search
options.C: It displays actions related to viewing and filtering the entire list
options.D: It displays actions such as view form, view related task, and add relationship

explanation: The Column Context Menu offers quick report creation, list configuration, and export options.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'view form, view related task, add relationship' option describes the related-list/record context menu, not the column header's context menu.

text: For Administrators creating new Service Catalog items, what is a characteristic they should know about Service Catalog variables?

options.A: Service Catalog variables can only be used in Record Producers
options.B: Service Catalog variables can only be used in Order Guides
options.C: Service Catalog variables cannot affect the order price
options.D: Service Catalog variables are global by default

explanation: Catalog variables defined directly on an item are global by default unless scoped via a Variable Set.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Cannot affect order price' is actually false the other way — variables CAN affect price. (This question is flagged low-confidence; verify against current ServiceNow docs.)

text: What is a schema map?

options.A: A schema map enables administrators to define records from specific tables as trouble sources for Configuration Items
options.B: A schema map graphically organizes the visual task boards for the CMDB
options.C: A schema map graphically displays the Configuration Items that support a business service
options.D: A schema map displays the details of tables and their relationships in a visual manner, allowing administrators to view and easily access different parts of the database schema

explanation: A schema map visually displays tables and their relationships so admins can explore the database schema.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'displays CIs supporting a business service' option actually describes a Business Service Map, a different (CMDB-specific) diagram — a schema map is about table/field relationships generally.

text: Two departments (HR Onboarding and Facilities) have come to you, asking for a way for employees to request event room set up services. The requirements are the same for the form and the task routing to the Facilities' assignment group. ✑ For HR, the item will be used primarily for the Onboarding coordinators, for employee orientation sessions. ✑ For Facilities, the item will be used for anyone in the company who needs room set up services. However, both departments have their own service catalogs. What do you do, to support these requirements?

options.A: Create one Catalog Item for HR Event Room Set Up and one for Facilities Event Room Set Up; then publish each to the appropriate Catalog.
options.B: Create one Catalog Item for Event Room Set Up; then publish to both Catalogs.
options.C: Create one Catalog Item for Event Room Set Up; then publish to the Parent Catalog, which is accessible to both HR and Facilities.
options.D: Create one Catalog Item for Event Room Set Up; then use ACLs to control access.

explanation: Create one Catalog Item and publish it to both catalogs (a catalog item can belong to multiple catalogs).
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'Parent Catalog' option invents a concept that isn't the standard mechanism — a catalog item's Catalogs related list simply supports being published to multiple catalogs directly.

text: What is the purpose of a Related List?

options.B: To dot-walk to a core table

explanation: A Related List presents records related to the current record.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'To create a one-to-many relationship' describes the underlying data model, but a Related List's actual purpose is presenting the related records themselves, not defining the relationship.

text: When creating a global custom table named `abc`, what is the table name that is automatically assigned by the platform?
explanation: A global custom table named 'abc' is automatically prefixed as u_abc.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'sys_' is reserved for ServiceNow's own system tables — custom global tables always get the 'u_' prefix.

text: Your customer would like to create a new template to notify users who are affected by network outages at their site. Which module would you use to create a new notification?
explanation: New notifications are created under System Notification > Email > Notifications.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Administration > Notification Overview' sounds plausible as an admin path, but new notifications are specifically created under System Notification > Email > Notifications.

text: Which ServiceNow products can be used to discover and populate the CMDB? (Choose two.)
explanation: Discovery and IntegrationHub ETL are ServiceNow products used to discover/populate the CMDB.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'CMDB Plug-in' just activates the CMDB tables/app — it doesn't discover or populate data the way Discovery and IntegrationHub ETL do.

text: Which of the following are not included in an Update Set, by default? (Choose four.)
explanation: By default, Update Sets do not capture Homepages, Data (records), Schedules, or Related List layout changes — these must be added manually.
wrongAnswerNotes: Business Rules and Client Scripts ARE captured by Update Sets by default — don't confuse genuine customizations with data/layout items that are excluded. (Flagged low-confidence — verify.)

text: Group records are stored in which table?
explanation: Group records are stored in the Group [sys_user_group] table.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'sn_' isn't the real prefix here — Groups live in the sys_user_group table.

text: On a related list, which buttons are commonly used for managing the records on the list? (Choose three.)
explanation: Related lists commonly use Add, Edit, and New buttons to manage records.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Publish' is a real button elsewhere in ServiceNow (like Knowledge articles) but isn't a standard related-list management button.

text: Which statement is true about business rules?

options.A: A business rule must run before a database action occurs
options.B: A business rule can be a piece of Javascript
options.C: A business rule must not run before a database action occurs
options.D: A business rule monitors fields on a form

explanation: A business rule can be a piece of server-side JavaScript.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Must run before a database action' is too absolute — business rules can run before, after, async, or on display, not only before.

text: The Employee On-boarding team has asked for a way for managers to order computers, monitors, business cards, and cell phones for new employees. How would you proceed to meet this requirement?
explanation: An Order Guide lets managers order multiple related items (computer, monitor, cards, phone) as one request.
wrongAnswerNotes: A Record Producer creates one task record from a form — an Order Guide is the right fit here since it bundles multiple catalog items into one request.

text: What section on a task record would you use to see the most recent update made to a record?
explanation: The Activity section of a task record shows the most recent updates/comments.
wrongAnswerNotes: The Audit Log is more of a technical field-change history — the Activity section is what actually surfaces the most recent update/comment feed.

text: In what order should filter elements be specified?
explanation: Filter elements are specified in the order Field, Operator, then Value.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Field, Operator, Condition' swaps Value for Condition — the real order is Field, Operator, Value.

text: Which modules can you use to create a new table? (Choose two.)
explanation: New tables can be created via the Dictionary module or the Tables module.
wrongAnswerNotes: Schema Map only visualizes existing tables — it doesn't let you create a new one.

text: What are three security modules often used by the System Administrator? (Choose three.)
explanation: Common admin security modules: System Properties > Security, System Security > Access Control (ACL), and High Security Settings.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'My Access' is an end-user self-service screen, not an admin security module.

text: Which banner icon do you use to change your personal system settings, like your instance color scheme?
explanation: The Gear icon opens personal system settings, including the instance color scheme.
wrongAnswerNotes: The question mark icon opens Help, not personal settings — that's the Gear icon.

text: IntegrationHub enables execution of third-party APIs as a part of a flow. These integrations are referred to as
explanation: Third-party API calls executed in a flow via IntegrationHub spokes are referred to as actions.
wrongAnswerNotes: A spoke is the package that PROVIDES the integration — the individual thing you drag into a flow is called an action.

text: What instance resource allows you to access guided tours, information about actions, and instructions on how to use inputs and outputs in your flow?
explanation: The Help Panel (question mark icon) gives guided tours and info on flow inputs/outputs/actions.
wrongAnswerNotes: Docs, Community, and Wiki are real general resources, but the specific in-flow guided tours live in the Help Panel (question mark icon).

text: What types of entities can receive task assignments, in ServiceNow? (Choose two.)
explanation: Both Users and Groups can receive task assignments.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Teams' isn't a ServiceNow task-assignment concept — only Users and Groups can be assigned.

text: In what order are Access Controls evaluated?

options.A: Field-level - most specific to most general; then Table-level - most specific to most general
options.B: Field-level - most general to most specific; then Row-level - most specific to most general
options.C: Table-level - most specific to most general; then Field-level - most specific to most general
options.D: Table-level - most specific to most general, then Row-level - most specific to most general

explanation: Access Controls are evaluated Table-level (specific to general), then Field-level (specific to general).
wrongAnswerNotes: The row-level-first option swaps in Row-level for Field-level — the real second phase is Field-level, not Row-level.

text: Which are valid Service Now User Authentication Methods? (Choose three.)
explanation: Valid authentication methods: Local database, LDAP, and SSO.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'XML feed' is an import/integration concept, not a user authentication method.

text: What feature can track the amount of time that a task has been open, to ensure that tasks are completed within an allotted time?
explanation: Service Level Agreements track how long a task has been open against an allotted time.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Task Escalation Clock' isn't a real ServiceNow feature name — the actual mechanism is a Service Level Agreement.

text: Which of the following statement describes the purpose of an Order Guide?

options.A: Order Guides restrict the number of items in an order to only one item per request
options.B: Order Guide provide a list of guidelines for Administrators on how to set up item variables
options.C: Order Guide provide the ability to order multiple, related items as one request
options.D: Order Guides take the user directly to the checkout without prompting for information

explanation: An Order Guide lets a user order multiple related items as a single request.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'restrict to one item' option is backwards — Order Guides exist specifically to let you order MULTIPLE items in one request, not restrict to one.

text: What is the Import Set Table?

options.A: A table where data will be placed, post-transformation
options.C: A staging area for imported records
options.D: A repository for Update Set information

explanation: The Import Set Table is a staging area for imported records before transformation.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'table where data placed post-transformation' option actually describes the TARGET table — the Import Set Table is the staging area BEFORE transformation, not after.

text: When searching using the App Navigator search field, what can be returned? (Choose four.)
explanation: App Navigator search returns application/module names, Favorites, History records, and dashboard gauge titles.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Names of Modules' and 'Names of Applications' are just narrower restatements of the combined Apps+Modules option — the four distinct return types are Apps/Modules, Favorites, History, and Dashboard Gauge titles. (Flagged low-confidence — verify.)

text: When importing data from a spreadsheet, which step defines where the incoming data columns will be written in the receiving table?
explanation: Creating the Transform Map is the step that defines where incoming spreadsheet columns are written on the target table.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Field Matching' describes the activity generically, but the actual named step/screen where this happens is Create Transform Map.

text: What needs to be specified, when creating a Business Rule? (Choose four.)
explanation: Creating a Business Rule requires specifying the Table, Script, Timing (When), and Condition.
wrongAnswerNotes: UI Action and Update Set aren't things you configure ON a Business Rule — the four required pieces are Table, Script, Timing, and Condition.

text: Which one of the following statements describes the purpose of a Service Catalog flow?

options.A: A Service Catalog flow generates three basic components: item variable types, tasks, and approvals
options.B: Although a Service Catalog flow cannot send notifications, the flow drives complex fulfillment processes
options.C: A Service Catalog flow is used to drive complex fulfillment processes and sends notifications to defined users or groups
options.D: A Service Catalog flow generates three basic components: item variable types, tasks, and notifications

explanation: A Service Catalog flow drives complex fulfillment and sends notifications to defined users/groups.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'cannot send notifications' option is backwards — Service Catalog flows absolutely CAN and do send notifications as part of fulfillment.

text: What feature allows you to limit who is able to contribute or read knowledge within a knowledge base?
explanation: User Criteria limits who can contribute to or read a knowledge base.
wrongAnswerNotes: Roles can layer on top, but the specific mechanism built for KB contribute/read restriction is User Criteria.

text: When you need to orchestrate business processes across services with little technical user knowledge, which utility would you use?
explanation: Flow Designer orchestrates business processes with little technical scripting knowledge required.
wrongAnswerNotes: Workflow Editor is the older, more technical legacy tool — Flow Designer is the low-code option built for less-technical users.

text: Which type of tables may be extended by other tables, but do not extend another table?
explanation: Base Tables can be extended by others but do not themselves extend another table.
wrongAnswerNotes: Extended Tables are the ones DOING the extending — the question asks about tables that only get extended, which is Base Tables.

text: What module would you use if you wanted to view a list of all of the fields on the Incident table? (Choose two.)
explanation: Tables & Columns and Dictionary both let you view all fields on the Incident table.
wrongAnswerNotes: Schema shows table relationships visually, not a flat list of fields — for that you need Dictionary or Tables & Columns.

text: While showing a customer their incident form, they ask to change the Priority values to display their internal terminology P1, P2, P3, P4. They want it to be consistent across all Tasks. How would you do that? Right click on Priority and select what?
explanation: Right-clicking a Priority choice field and configuring its choice list lets you relabel values consistently across Tasks.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Show Choice List' is a very close-sounding decoy to the actual menu option 'Show Choices'. (Flagged low-confidence — verify the exact menu wording.)

text: Which of the following allows a user to edit field values in a list without opening the form?
explanation: List Editor allows inline editing of field values in a list without opening the form.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Data Editor' isn't a real ServiceNow feature name — inline list editing is done via the List Editor.

text: On a filter condition, there is an element, which is based on the table, the user access rights, and columns on the table. What is this element called?
explanation: A Field is the filter element tied to the table, user access rights, and column.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Data Element' sounds technical but isn't the real term here — the filter builder calls this a Field.

text: A new Service Desk employee in Latin America complains that the create dates and times are incorrect on their Incident list. What would you suggest to fix this issue?

options.B: Have them use the gear icon to set the employee's time zone
options.C: Recommend they use Chrome, instead of Explorer.
options.D: Use the system properties to correct the instance's time zone.
options.E: Have them correct the time zone on their computer.

explanation: Each user sets their own time zone via the Gear icon / user preferences to correct displayed date/times.
wrongAnswerNotes: Correcting the instance's time zone would fix it for this one user but break it for everyone else on the shared instance — time zone should be set per-user, not instance-wide.

text: How is a user defined in ServiceNow?

options.A: A user is a record stored in the Profile [sys_user_profile] table
options.B: A user is a record stored in the User [sys_user] table
options.C: A user is a record stored in the User Preference [sys_user_preference] table
options.D: A user is a field in the LDAP integration

explanation: A user is a record stored in the User [sys_user] table.
wrongAnswerNotes: sys_user_profile isn't the core user record — the actual User record lives in sys_user.

text: How is a group defined in ServiceNow?

options.A: A group is one record stored in the Group Type [sys_user_group_type] table
options.B: A group is one record stored in the Group [sys_user_group] table
options.C: A group defines a set of users that share the same location
options.D: A group defines a set of users that share the same job title

explanation: A group is a record stored in the Group [sys_user_group] table.
wrongAnswerNotes: sys_user_group_type is a different supporting table — the actual Group record itself lives in sys_user_group.

text: On what part of the ServiceNow instance, would you find the option to access applications, like Incident Management?
explanation: The Application Navigator is where you access applications like Incident Management.
wrongAnswerNotes: Service Desk Homepage and Self Service Module are landing pages, not the general mechanism for browsing all applications — that's the Application Navigator.

text: Which module is used as the first step for importing data?
explanation: Load Data is the first module used when starting a data import.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Import Data' isn't the actual first module name — the real first step is called Load Data.

text: What are examples of UI Actions, relating to Lists? (Choose four.)
explanation: List-related UI Actions include List Links, List Choices, List Buttons, and List Context Menu.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'List Override' and 'List Control' aren't real UI Action category names — don't be fooled by plausible-sounding technical terms. (Flagged low-confidence — verify.)

text: What function do you use to add buttons, links, and context menu items on forms and lists?
explanation: UI Actions add buttons, links, and context menu items to forms and lists.
wrongAnswerNotes: UI Policies control field behavior (mandatory/read-only/visible), not buttons/links/menu items — that's the classic UI Policy vs UI Action mix-up.

text: On a form, which type of field has this icon which can be clicked, to see a preview of the associated record? (i icon)
explanation: Reference fields show an (i) preview icon for the associated record.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Lookup' sounds descriptive but isn't the actual ServiceNow field type name — the preview-icon field type is called Reference.

text: The Report Designer contains different sections for configuring your report. Which section is used to specify grouping and calculations to be run against the data?
explanation: The Group By section of Report Designer specifies grouping and calculations.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Data' is where you pick the source table, not where grouping/calculations are configured — that's the Group By section specifically.

text: When working on a form, what is the difference between Insert and Update operations?

options.A: Insert creates a new record and Update saves changes, both remain on the form
options.B: Insert creates a new record and Update saves changes, both exit the form
options.C: Insert saves changes and exits the form, Update saves changes and remains on the form
options.D: Insert saves changes and remains on the form, Update saves changes and exits the form

explanation: Insert (Submit) saves and exits the form; Update saves changes and keeps you on the form.
wrongAnswerNotes: The option assuming both operations remain on the form is wrong for Insert — Insert (Submit) exits to the list, only Update keeps you there.

text: Which tool is used to define relationships between fields in an import set table and a target table?
explanation: Transform Map defines the field relationships between an import set table and the target table.
wrongAnswerNotes: Schema Map shows overall table relationships in the CMDB sense — the specific import-to-target field mapping tool is Transform Map.

text: When testing a catalog item, having a manager approval flows, which of these best practices would you follow? (Choose three.)

options.A: Make sure the latest flows are activated.
options.B: Use the instance Incognito setting to quickly toggle between requester and approver.
options.C: Impersonate the requester to ensure the form works.
options.D: Make sure the requester's user record has a manager specified.
options.E: Create and select your Testing Update Set, before starting the test cases.
options.F: Use your Admin account, so you can approve the items quickly.

explanation: Best practice: ensure the latest flows are active, impersonate the requester to test the form, and confirm the requester's manager is set (needed for manager approval).
wrongAnswerNotes: Using your Admin account to approve quickly short-circuits the actual test — you want to impersonate the real approver, not bypass the flow with admin rights.

text: What is a characteristic of importing data into ServiceNow?

options.A: An existing Transform Map can be used one time on the same import set
options.B: Coalesce fields are used only after running Transform
options.C: Any user can manage and set up import sets
options.D: An existing Transform Map can be used multiple times on the same import set

explanation: An existing Transform Map can be reused multiple times on the same import set.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'used one time' option is backwards — Transform Maps are explicitly reusable across multiple import runs on the same set, not limited to one use.

text: Which ServiceNow capability provides assistance to help users obtain information, make decisions, and perform common work tasks via a messaging interface?
explanation: Virtual Agent provides conversational assistance for information, decisions, and common tasks.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Chat bot' is a generic description, not ServiceNow's actual product name — the specific capability is called Virtual Agent.

text: When building an extended table from a base table, which fields do you need to create? (Choose two.)

options.A: The fields that are not in the base table.
options.B: The mandatory fields for the base table.
options.C: The fields that are specific to the extended table.
options.D: The reference fields for the base table.

explanation: When extending a base table you only need to create fields not already in the base table / specific to the extended table.
wrongAnswerNotes: The mandatory-fields and reference-fields options describe fields already inherited automatically from the base table — you only need to create fields that are new/specific to the extension.

text: Which tool is used for creating dependencies between configuration items in the CMDB?
explanation: The CI Relationship Editor is used to create dependencies between CIs in the CMDB.
wrongAnswerNotes: CMDB Class Manager manages CI classes/attributes, not the relationships between individual CIs — that's the CI Relationship Editor.

text: Which of the following statements describes how data is organized in a table?

options.A: A column is a field in the database and a record is one user
options.B: A column is one field and a record is one row
options.C: A column is one field and a record is one column
options.D: A column contains data from one user and a record is one set of fields

explanation: A column is one field, and a record is one row.
wrongAnswerNotes: The options tying a 'record' specifically to a user are wrong — generically, a record is simply one row, regardless of what table it's in.

text: As an IT employee what interface would you use, if you wanted to browse internal IT documentation, like troubleshooting scripts and FAQs?
explanation: The Knowledge application is used to browse internal IT documentation like troubleshooting scripts and FAQs.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'ServiceNow Wiki' isn't the built-in feature being described — internal docs/FAQs live in the Knowledge application.

text: What catalog tool would you use to create a catalog item or record producer?
explanation: Catalog Builder is the tool used to create a catalog item or record producer.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Catalog Designer' is a near-identical sounding decoy — the actual tool name is Catalog Builder.

text: What are the steps to retrieve an Update Set?

options.A: Verify Update Set is Complete, Retrieve, Preview, Apply
options.B: Verify Update Set is Complete, Test Connection, Apply
options.C: Verify Update Set is Complete, Test Connection, Commit
options.D: Verify Update Set is Complete, Retrieve, Preview, Commit

explanation: Retrieving an Update Set follows: verify complete, Retrieve, Preview, Commit.
wrongAnswerNotes: The '...Retrieve, Preview, Apply' option is a near-miss — the final step is specifically named Commit, not Apply.

text: On the Reports page, what sections allow you to see which reports are visible to different audiences? (Choose four.)
explanation: The Reports page shows visibility via Group, My reports, Global, and All sections.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Team' and 'Company' sound like plausible visibility scopes but aren't real section names — the actual four are Group, My reports, Global, and All.

text: How are Workflows moved between instances?

options.A: Workflows are moved using Update Sets
options.B: Workflows are moved using Transform Maps
options.C: Workflows are moved using Application Sets
options.D: Workflows cannot be moved between instances

explanation: Workflows are moved between instances using Update Sets.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Cannot be moved' is a common misconception — Workflows absolutely can move between instances, via Update Sets like other customizations.

text: Access Control rules are applied to a specific table, like the Incident table. What is the object name for a rule that is specific to the Incident table and the Major Incident field?
explanation: The ACL object name uses dot notation: incident.major_incident.
wrongAnswerNotes: The capitalized 'Incident.Major_Incident' option uses the right concept but wrong casing/format — ACL object names use lowercase dot notation.

text: What is a no-code approach to control the mandatory or read-only state of a form field?
explanation: UI Policy is the no-code way to control mandatory/read-only field state.
wrongAnswerNotes: Client Script can control mandatory/read-only too but requires scripting — the no-code specific answer is UI Policy.

text: What is a Record Producer?

options.A: A Record Producer is a type of Catalog Item that is used for Requests, not Services
options.B: A Record Producer creates user records
options.C: A Record Producer is a type of Catalog Item that provides easy ordering by bundling requests
options.D: A Record Producer is a type of a Catalog Item that allows users to create task-based records from the Service Catalog

explanation: A Record Producer is a Catalog Item type that lets users create task-based records from the Service Catalog.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'bundling requests' option actually describes an Order Guide's job — a Record Producer's specific role is generating task-based records, not bundling multiple items.

text: Which one of the following statements is a recommendation from ServiceNow about Update Sets?

options.A: Avoid using the Default Update set as an Update Set for moving customizations from instance to instance
options.B: Before moving customizations from instance to instance with Update Sets, ensure that both instances are different versions
options.C: Use the Baseline Update Set to store the contents of items after they are changed the first time
options.D: Once an Update Set is closed as "Complete", change it back to "In Progress" until it is applied to another instance

explanation: ServiceNow recommends avoiding the Default Update Set when moving customizations between instances.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Baseline Update Set' isn't a real recommended practice — the actual guidance is to avoid the Default Update Set specifically.

text: Which ServiceNow resource is a framework that ensures the data your ServiceNow application requires maps correctly to the appropriate CMDB tables?
explanation: The Common Service Data Model (CSDM) maps application data correctly to CMDB tables.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'CMDBCM' and 'CICM' are invented-sounding acronyms — the real framework name is CSDM.

text: Which of the following are a type of client scripts supported in ServiceNow? (Choose four.)
explanation: Valid client script types: onLoad, onChange, onSubmit, onCellEdit.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'onUpdate' and 'onSave' sound plausible by naming pattern but aren't real client script types — the four real ones are onLoad, onChange, onSubmit, onCellEdit.

text: Which database provides a logical model of your company infrastructure by identifying, controlling, maintaining and verifying CIs that exist?
explanation: The CMDB provides the logical model identifying, controlling, maintaining, and verifying CIs.
wrongAnswerNotes: CSDM is the framework/model that shapes the CMDB's data — but the actual database being described here is the CMDB itself.

text: What is a key difference between Reporting and Performance Analytics?

options.A: Performance Analytics contains snapshots of data taken over time; Reporting shows only the data as it is, at the moment the report is run.
options.B: Performance Analytics can show trends; Reports cannot
options.C: Reports can be run on a scheduled basis; Performance Analytics cannot.
options.D: Performance Analytics data can be published to Dashboards; Reports cannot.
options.E: Performance Analytics shows KPIs; Reporting does not.

explanation: Performance Analytics stores time-based snapshots; Reporting shows only current data.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'shows KPIs; Reporting does not' option overstates things — Reporting can show basic metrics too; the real defining difference is PA's historical snapshots vs Reporting's point-in-time data.

text: What is the path an Administrator could take to view the fulfillment stage task list for an order placed by a user?
explanation: The fulfillment path is REQ > RITM > TASK.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'PROCUREMENT' isn't a real ServiceNow record type in this chain — the fulfillment path ends at TASK, not a procurement record.

text: When moving a homepage or dashboard between instances, what must you remember?

options.A: Manually add them to the update set
options.B: They cannot be moved via update set
options.C: They are automatically added to the update set
options.D: Create a separate update set for them

explanation: Homepages/dashboards must be manually added to an Update Set; they are not captured automatically.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'automatically added' option is backwards — homepages/dashboards are NOT automatically captured; you must add them to the Update Set yourself.

text: What import utility do you use when the field names on the import set match the name of the fields on the Target table?
explanation: Automatic Mapping is used when import set field names match target table field names.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Schema Mapping' isn't the real import utility name here — when field names already match, you use Automatic Mapping.

text: What do you need to do before you can use an Application-based trigger in your flow?

options.C: Activate application spoke, and plug-ins as needed
options.D: Assign Application trigger role [sn_app_trigger_write] to SME

explanation: Before using an application-based trigger you must activate the relevant application spoke and plugins.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Activate application trigger spoke' is close but incomplete — you need the application's spoke AND its plugins activated, not just a generic 'trigger spoke'.

text: Which ServiceNow utility gives a Service Desk agent the ability to trace from a Service having an issue, to see which CIs supporting that service have active issues?
explanation: CI Dependency View lets a Service Desk agent trace from a Service to CIs with active issues.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'CI Health Dashboard' sounds relevant but isn't the tool for tracing service-to-CI issues — that's specifically the CI Dependency View.

text: On the CI Dependency View, what enables you to trace from an infrastructure item, like a Server, to the Services that are dependent on that Server?
explanation: Relationships defined between CIs enable tracing from an infrastructure item to dependent Services.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Service Tracer' isn't a real ServiceNow feature — the tracing capability comes from defined CI Relationships themselves.

text: How would you distinguish between a Base Class table and a Parent Class table?

options.A: Extended tables are always extended from Parent tables. Extended tables are usually extended from Base tables.
options.B: Extended tables can be extended from Parent tables or Base tables; but they cannot be extended from both.
options.C: Base Class tables always have tables extended from them. Parent tables do not have tables extended from them.
options.D: Base Class table is not extended from another table, Parent class tables may be extended from another table.

explanation: A Base Class table isn't extended from another table; a Parent Class table may itself be extended from another.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'always have tables extended from them' option is too absolute — a Base Class table doesn't NEED to have anything extended from it to still be a Base table; the real distinguishing fact is whether IT extends something else.

text: The Report Designer contains different sections for configuring your report. Which section is used to adjust the look of your report, including colors, titles and legend layout?
explanation: The Style section of Report Designer adjusts colors, titles, and legend layout.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Layout' isn't a real Report Designer section name — colors/titles/legend are all under the Style section.

text: Which of the following steps can be used to import new data into ServiceNow from a spreadsheet?

options.B: Load Data, Create Transform Map, Run Transform
options.C: Define Data Source, Select Transform Map, Run Transform
options.D: Select Import Set, Select Transform Map, Run Transform

explanation: Importing new spreadsheet data follows: Load Data, Create Transform Map, Run Transform.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'Select Import Set...' option skips the crucial first step — you must Load the Data before you can create/select a Transform Map.

text: When a user reports that they are not able to see modules on the application navigator, what can you do, to see what modules are visible to them?

options.A: Look up their password, so you can login with their account

explanation: Impersonating the user shows exactly what modules are visible to them.
wrongAnswerNotes: Looking up their password is a serious security no-no — Impersonation exists specifically so you never need their credentials.

text: What type of user (persona) has clearly defined paths and workflows in the platform and have one or more roles (ie itil and approver_user)?
explanation: A 'Process User' persona follows clearly defined paths/workflows tied to specific roles (e.g. itil, approver_user).
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Request Fulfiller' describes a job function, not the formal persona term — the official ServiceNow term for this defined-path user type is Process User.

text: Which one of the following is an accurate list of changes that are captured in an Update Set?

options.A: Changes made to: tables, forms, schedules, and client scripts
options.B: Changes made to: tables, forms, Business Rules, and data records
options.C: Changes made to: tables, forms, groups, and configuration items (CIs)
options.D: Changes made to: table, forms, views, and fields

explanation: Update Sets capture schema-level customizations like tables, forms, views, and fields — not data records.
wrongAnswerNotes: The option listing 'tables, forms, Business Rules, data records' is a near-miss that wrongly includes 'data records' — Update Sets never capture actual data/records, only schema-level customizations like fields and views.

text: From a related list, what would a user click to personalize the layout of the columns?
explanation: The Gear icon at the bottom of a related list personalizes its column layout.
wrongAnswerNotes: Pencil sounds like an intuitive edit icon, but the actual related-list personalize control is the Gear icon.

text: You are showing your customer a new form that you have created for their new application. They would like to add a field to the form. Where could you do that? (Choose two.)

options.B: Right click on form header, select Configure > Form Layout
options.C: Click on context menu, select Configure > Form Designer

explanation: Adding a field to a form is done via Configure > Form Layout or Configure > Form Design.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Fields and Columns module' and 'Field Class Manager' aren't real navigation paths — the two real routes are via Configure > Form Layout or Configure > Form Design.

text: After finishing your work on High Security Settings, what do you do to return to normal admin security levels?

options.C: Use System Administration > Normal Security module

explanation: Logging out and back in returns an admin session to normal security levels after High Security Settings work.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'End Impersonation' is for a different feature entirely — exiting High Security elevation isn't the same as ending an impersonation session. (Flagged low-confidence — verify the exact steps.)

text: Which of the following is true of Service Catalog Items in relation to the Service Catalog?
explanation: Service Catalog Items are the building blocks of the Service Catalog.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'They provide options' sounds similar but is too generic — the specific, textbook-correct framing is that Catalog Items are the Service Catalog's building blocks.

text: What do you call any component that needs to be managed in order to deliver services?
explanation: A Configuration Item is any component that needs to be managed to deliver services.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Asset' is a related but distinct concept (financial/procurement tracking) — the thing actually 'managed to deliver services' is a Configuration Item.

text: What is the difference between a UI Policy and Data Policy?

options.A: Data Policies run when data is entered through the form, by an Import Set, or by web services, while UI Policies are set only by web services
options.B: Data Policies can be converted into UI Policies, but UI Policies cannot be converted into Data Policies
options.C: Data Policies run regardless of how data is entered into ServiceNow, while UI Policies are used for form interactions
options.D: Data Policies run only after UI Policies run successfully

explanation: Data Policies run regardless of entry method; UI Policies apply specifically to form interactions.
wrongAnswerNotes: The option claiming Data Policies only apply via web services reverses the real distinction — UI Policies are for FORM interactions specifically, while Data Policies are the ones that apply regardless of entry channel.

text: Which term refers to application menus and modules which you may want to access quickly and often?
explanation: A Favorite is an application menu/module you want to access quickly and often.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Bookmark' is a natural-sounding synonym, but ServiceNow's specific term for quick-access menus/modules is Favorite.

text: What is a Notification?

options.A: A new Knowledge article created by a Business Rule
options.B: A tool for alerting users that events that concern them have occurred
options.C: A message through Connect related to a Change Request

explanation: A Notification alerts users about events that concern them.
wrongAnswerNotes: The Change-Request-specific option describes one narrow example, not the general definition — a Notification broadly alerts users to any event that concerns them.

text: What type of field is Boolean and appears as a check box?
explanation: The True/False field type is a Boolean that displays as a checkbox.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Yes/No' describes the same idea in plain language, but ServiceNow's actual internal field type name is True/False.

text: The baseline Service Catalog homepage contains links to which of the following components?

options.A: Record Producers, Order Guides, and Catalog Items
options.B: Order Guides, Item Variables, and flows
options.C: Order Guides, Catalog Items, and flows
options.D: Record Producers, Order Guides, and Item Variables

explanation: The baseline Service Catalog homepage links to Record Producers, Order Guides, and Catalog Items.
wrongAnswerNotes: The option swapping in 'Item Variables' for 'Catalog Items' is wrong — the baseline homepage links to Record Producers, Order Guides, AND Catalog Items.

text: Access Control rules may provide access security for which of the following database objects?

options.A: For a specific role, group, or user
options.B: For a specific row, column, or table
options.D: For a specific CMDB Configuration item

explanation: ACLs secure a specific row, column (field), or table.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'role, group, or user' option describes the 'who' (granted access), but the question asks what's being secured — that's rows, columns, or tables.

text: A task worker asks how they can monitor any updates occurring to records assigned to him, like responses from customers. What do you suggest?

options.A: On My Work list, select the Activity Stream icon to show a frame with live updates
options.B: Click on the eyeglass icon to expand the Monitor frame
options.C: Open an Agent workspace tab for each record he wants to monitor
options.D: Select Service Desk > My Work Dashboard

explanation: The Activity Stream icon on My Work list shows a live-updating frame of recent record activity.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'My Work Dashboard' isn't the specific live-update feature — that's the Activity Stream icon directly on the My Work list.

text: Which features allow you to update multiple records at one time? (Choose two.)
explanation: List Editor and the Update Selected action allow updating multiple records at once.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Bulk Record Update' and 'Data Remediation Dashboard' sound official but aren't real ServiceNow features — the two real tools are List Editor and Update Selected.

text: A knowledge article must be which of the following states to display to a user?
explanation: A knowledge article must be Published to display to a user.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Reviewed' sounds like a plausible pre-publish state, but only the Published state actually makes an article visible to users.

text: Which module is used to access the knowledge bases which are available to you?
explanation: Knowledge > Knowledge Bases is the module listing the knowledge bases available to you.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Knowledge > Home' is a plausible-sounding landing page name, but the module that specifically lists all your accessible KBs is Knowledge > Knowledge Bases.

text: What is a sys_id?

options.A: Unique 32-character identifier that is assigned to every record
options.D: Unique 64-character identifier that is assigned to every record

explanation: sys_id is a unique 32-character identifier assigned to every record.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 64-character option swaps in the wrong length — sys_id is specifically 32 characters, a classic numeric decoy to watch for.

text: Which term best describes something that is created, has worked performed upon it, and is eventually moved to a state of closed?
explanation: A task is created, worked on, and eventually moved to a closed state.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Event' is a trigger mechanism, not a thing that itself gets worked on and closed — that lifecycle describes a task.

text: Which of the following protects applications by identifying and restricting access to available files and data?
explanation: Access Control Rules protect applications by restricting access to files and data.
wrongAnswerNotes: Application Scope isolates one app's code/data from others, but the broader mechanism restricting file/data access generally is Access Control Rules.

text: What is a role in ServiceNow?

options.A: A role is one record in the Role [user_sys_role] table
options.B: A role is a set of modules for a particular application
options.C: A role is one record in the Role [sys_user_role] table
options.D: A role is a persona used in Live Feed Chat

explanation: A role is one record in the Role [sys_user_role] table.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'user_sys_role' scrambles the real table name's word order — it's sys_user_role, not user_sys_role.

text: Which module would you use to create a new automation of business logic such as approvals, tasks, and notifications?
explanation: Process Automation > Flow Designer creates new business logic automation (approvals, tasks, notifications).
wrongAnswerNotes: Workflow Editor is the legacy tool — the current module for NEW automation builds is Flow Designer.

text: What is the name of the table relationship, where two or more tables are related in a bi-directional relationship, so that the related records are visible from both tables in a related list?
explanation: Many to Many is the bi-directional relationship visible from both tables in a related list.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Database View' is a reporting construct joining tables, not the relationship TYPE itself — bi-directional visibility from both sides describes Many to Many.

text: Which feature enables business process owners to organize Flow Designer content into unified and digitized cross-enterprise processes via a digitized task board interface?
explanation: Process Automation Designer organizes Flow Designer content into cross-enterprise processes via a digitized task board.
wrongAnswerNotes: Flow Designer is the underlying building block, but the feature organizing that content into cross-enterprise task-board processes is specifically Process Automation Designer.

text: Which one of the following statements best describes the purpose of an Update Set?

options.A: An Update Set allows administrators to group a series of changes into a named set and then move this set as a unit to other systems
options.B: By default, an Update Set includes customizations, Business Rules, and homepages
options.C: An Update Set is a group of customizations that is moved from Production to Development
options.D: By default, the changes included in an Update Set are visible only in the instance to which they are applied

explanation: An Update Set groups a series of changes into a named set to move as a unit to other systems.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'visible only in instance applied' option describes a side detail, not the core purpose — an Update Set's defining job is bundling and moving changes as one unit.

text: Where can Admins check which release is running on an ServiceNow instance?
explanation: The Stats module shows instance/build/release information.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'System.upgraded table' sounds specific and technical, but the actual place admins check release/version info is the Stats module.

text: On the Form header, which icon do you use to access form templates?
explanation: The Stamp icon on the Form header accesses form templates.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'More Options (...)' is a catch-all menu, but form templates specifically live behind the Stamp icon.

text: What would NOT appear in the Application Navigator if `service` is typed into the filter field?
explanation: 'Incident > Assigned to me' doesn't contain the text 'service', so it would not appear in a filter for 'service'.
wrongAnswerNotes: Watch the exact text match — the other options all literally contain the substring 'service' somewhere in their path, so only 'Incident > Assigned to me' truly lacks it.

text: What would you do, on a list, if you wanted to show the records in groups, based on the column category? (Choose two.)

options.A: On list Context Menu, select Group By > Category
options.B: On the Filter Menu, select Group By > Category
options.C: Click Group On icon, select Category
options.D: On Navigator Filter, type tablename.group.category and press enter
options.E: On the Category column title, click Context menu > Group By Category

explanation: Grouping a list by a column category can be done via the List Context Menu or the column's own context menu, both offering Group By.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Group On icon' isn't a real ServiceNow UI element — grouping is done via context menus (list-level or column-level), not a dedicated icon.

text: What is a Dictionary Override?

options.A: A Dictionary Override is an incoming customer update in an Update Set which applies to the same objects as a newer local customer update
options.B: A Dictionary Override is the addition, modification, or removal of anything that could have an effect on IT services
options.C: A Dictionary Override is a task within a flow that requests an action before the flow can continue
options.D: A Dictionary Override sets field properties in extended tables

explanation: A Dictionary Override sets field properties specifically on an extended table.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'incoming update applies to same objects as newer local update' option actually describes an Update Set collision scenario, a different concept entirely — a Dictionary Override specifically sets field properties on an extended table.

text: When moving multiple update sets at one time, what might you do to facilitate the move?
explanation: Batching lets you move multiple Update Sets together.
wrongAnswerNotes: Preview is a real per-Update-Set action, but moving several AT ONCE is specifically called Batching.

text: Buttons, form links, and context menu items are all examples of what type of functionality?
explanation: Buttons, form links, and context menu items are all UI Actions.
wrongAnswerNotes: Business Rule runs server-side logic on record changes — visible UI elements like buttons/links/menu items are specifically UI Actions.

text: When designing a flow, how do you reference data from a record, in that flow?

options.A: Drag the table icon onto the flow definition
options.B: Use the condition builder to specify the desired values
options.C: Specify the source table on the data pill related list
options.D: Drag the data pill onto the flow definition
options.E: Add the table reference using the slush bucket

explanation: You reference record data in a flow by dragging the data pill onto the flow definition.
wrongAnswerNotes: The condition builder is used for filters/conditions, not for referencing record data within a flow — that's done by dragging the data pill.

text: An IT user calls the service desk because they need to work on task records. All they can see is Self Service on their homepage when they login to the ServiceNow instance. What issue could explain this? (Choose two.)

options.A: Their user account does not have itil role
options.B: Their user account was not approved by their manager
options.C: Their user account is not logged in properly
options.D: Their user account failed LDAP authentication
options.E: Their user account does not belong to any groups, which contain the itil role

explanation: Missing itil role, or not belonging to any group that grants itil, explains why only Self Service is visible.
wrongAnswerNotes: A failed LDAP login would prevent access altogether, not just limit them to Self Service — the actual cause is a missing itil role or group membership.

text: What is the platform name for the User table?
explanation: sys_user is the platform table name for the User table.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'sys_users' is a plural near-miss — the real table name is singular: sys_user.

text: As it relates to ServiceNow reporting, which of the following statements describes what a metric can do?

options.A: A metric is a report gauge used on homepages to display real-time data
options.B: A metric is a time measurement used to report the effectiveness of workflows and SLAs
options.C: A metric is used to measure and evaluate the effectiveness of IT service management processes
options.D: A metric is a comparative measurement used to report the effectiveness of flows and SLAs.

explanation: A metric measures and evaluates the effectiveness of ITSM processes.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'workflows and SLAs' option narrows 'metric' too much — the broader, correct definition is measuring/evaluating ITSM process effectiveness generally.

text: What icon do you use to change the label on a Favorite?
explanation: The Pencil icon lets you change the label on a Favorite.
wrongAnswerNotes: The Star marks/represents the Favorite itself — to actually rename its label you click the Pencil icon.

text: Which module would you use to customize your instances banner image, text and colors?
explanation: System Properties > Basic Configuration UI16 customizes the instance banner image, text, and colors.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'System Properties > Branding' sounds like the obvious path but isn't the real module name — it's specifically Basic Configuration UI16.

text: What are the steps for importing data using an import set?

options.A: Select source file; Run automap; Transform data; Clean up target table
options.B: Identity source; Import transform map; Run transformer, Verify import
options.C: Setup LDAP; Test map; Create update set; Run import; Apply update set
options.D: Load the data; Create transform map; Transform data; Clean up import table

explanation: Import set steps: Load the data, Create transform map, Transform data, Clean up import table.
wrongAnswerNotes: The option inventing 'Run automap' as a step is wrong — the real sequence is Load the data, Create transform map, Transform data, Clean up import table.

text: Which icon would you double click, to expand and collapse the list of all Applications and Modules?
explanation: Double-clicking the Application icon expands/collapses the full Applications and Modules list.
wrongAnswerNotes: Star is associated with Favorites, not the App/Module expand-all toggle. (Flagged low-confidence — double check this specific interaction.)

text: A new service catalog item is being developed, but should only be visible to managers inside the HR Department. What method would you use to fulfill this requirement?

options.A: Specify the Dept_Mgr role on the catalog content block
options.B: Add the Department Manager group to the catalog item's user criteria
options.C: Add the Department Manager group to the catalog item's ACL
options.D: Only publish the item in the HR service catalog
options.E: Use a Dept_Mgr ACL on the HR service catalog

explanation: Adding the Department Manager group to the catalog item's User Criteria restricts visibility to HR managers.
wrongAnswerNotes: Specifying a role directly isn't how catalog item visibility is controlled — the real mechanism is adding the group to the item's User Criteria.

text: When using the Load Data and Transform Map process, what is the Mapping Assist used for?

options.A: Mapping fields using the Import Log
options.D: Mapping fields using a Field Map

explanation: Mapping Assist maps fields using a visual Field Map during Load Data/Transform Map.
wrongAnswerNotes: The Import Log just records what happened during import — it's not the tool used to actually map fields; that's the Field Map via Mapping Assist.

text: From the User menu, which actions can a user select? (Choose three.)
