explanation: The User menu offers Log Out, Elevate Roles, and Impersonate Users.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Approve Records' sounds like a natural User menu item, but approvals are handled elsewhere (your approvals list) — not from the User menu itself.

text: You have been asked to create a way for users to order a new iPhone, but only if they get two levels of approval. The approvers and users should be automatically notified at each approval level. What feature would you use to manage the approvals and notifications?
explanation: Flows (Flow Designer) manage multi-level approvals with automatic notifications at each stage.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Approval Chains' sounds like an official feature name but isn't a distinct ServiceNow product — multi-level approvals with notifications are built using Flows.

text: What is the name of the conversational bot platform that provides assistance to help users obtain information, make decisions, and perform common tasks?
explanation: Virtual Agent is ServiceNow's conversational bot platform.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Answer Agent' is an invented-sounding decoy — the real conversational bot platform is Virtual Agent.

text: Which technique is used to get information from a series of referenced fields from different tables?
explanation: Dot-Walking retrieves information from a chain of referenced fields across tables.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Table-Walking' sounds like a natural analog but isn't the real term — ServiceNow specifically calls this technique Dot-Walking.

text: Create Incident, Password Reset, and Report outage: what do these services in the Service Catalog have in common?

options.A: They direct the user to a record producer
options.B: They direct the user to a catalog property
options.C: They direct the user to a catalog UI policy
options.D: They direct the user to a catalog client script

explanation: Create Incident, Password Reset, and Report Outage all direct the user to a record producer.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Catalog property' isn't the shared mechanism here — all three of those catalog items route through a Record Producer.

text: What is the language used for scripting in ServiceNow?
explanation: JavaScript is the scripting language used throughout ServiceNow.
wrongAnswerNotes: The other languages (C++, PHP, Python) are all real languages but none are what ServiceNow scripting actually uses — it's JavaScript throughout.

text: On a Form header, what is the three bar icon called?
explanation: The three-bar icon on a Form header is the Additional Actions / Context Menu.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Hamburger icon' is the common colloquial name for this icon shape, but ServiceNow's official term for it on the Form header is Additional Actions/Context Menu.

text: What information does the System Dictionary contain?

options.A: The human-readable labels and language settings
options.B: The definition for each table and column
options.C: The information on how tables relate to each other
options.D: The language dictionary used for spell checking

explanation: The System Dictionary contains the definition for each table and column.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'labels and language settings' option describes translation/label management specifically, a narrower piece — the System Dictionary broadly defines every table and column.

text: To apply a UI Policy to all views, which field should be set to true in its definition record?
explanation: Setting Global to true applies a UI Policy across all views.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Inherit' sounds conceptually related but isn't the actual field name — the checkbox that applies a UI Policy to all views is specifically named Global.

text: What module in the Service Catalog application does an Administrator access to begin creating a new item?
explanation: Maintain Items is the module an admin uses to begin creating a new Service Catalog item.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Items' sounds like the obvious simpler name, but the actual admin module to begin creating a new catalog item is Maintain Items.

text: ServiceNow contains a resource which provides the following: ✑ A standard and shared set of service related definitions across ServiceNow products and platform that will enable and support true service level reporting. ✑ A CMDB framework across our products and platform that will enable and support multiple configuration strategies. What resource do these statements describe?
explanation: CSDM provides a shared service data model and CMDB framework across ServiceNow products.
wrongAnswerNotes: CMDB is the database CSDM helps structure — but the described 'framework across products' role belongs to CSDM itself, not the CMDB.

text: Which of the following is used to categorize, flag, and locate records?
explanation: Tags are used to categorize, flag, and locate records.
wrongAnswerNotes: Favorites is for quick access to menus/modules, not for categorizing/flagging arbitrary records — that's what Tags do.

text: What access does a user need to be able to import articles to a knowledge base?
explanation: A user needs 'Can contribute' access to import articles into a knowledge base.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'sn_knowledge_contribute' sounds like a plausible role name by pattern, but the actual documented access level required is 'Can contribute'.

text: What is generated from the Service Catalog once a user places an order for an item or service?
explanation: Placing an order from the Service Catalog generates a Request.
wrongAnswerNotes: A Change Request is a completely different ITSM process — ordering from the Service Catalog specifically generates a Request (REQ).

text: Categories in the knowledge base, by default, can be created and edited by which knowledge workers? (Choose two.)
explanation: By default, Knowledge Managers and Knowledge Category Managers can create/edit knowledge base categories.
wrongAnswerNotes: Knowledge Authors write articles but don't manage categories by default — that's specifically Knowledge Managers and Knowledge Category Managers.

text: What module enables an administrator to define destinations for imported data on any ServiceNow table?
explanation: Transform Map lets an admin define destinations for imported data on any table.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Field Transform' sounds like a subset of the real feature — the actual module for defining import destinations on any table is Transform Map.

text: Which core table in the ServiceNow platform provides a series of standard fields used on each of the tables that extend it, such as the Incident [incident] and Problem [problem] tables?
explanation: The Task [task] table provides the standard fields inherited by Incident, Problem, and other extended tables.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Assignment' sounds ITSM-relevant and could plausibly be a shared base table, but the actual table providing standard fields to Incident/Problem/etc. is Task.

text: What are two ways to generate an Event? (Choose two.)
explanation: Events can be generated from a Business Rule or a Workflow.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Knowledge article publication' sounds like a plausible event source, but the two documented ways to generate an Event are from a Business Rule or a Workflow.

text: While on an Incident record, how would you add a Tag for "Special Handling" to the record?

options.A: Click on the Context menu, select Add Tag, type Special Handling, press enter
options.B: Click on the More options (...) icon, click Add Tag, type Special Handling, press enter
options.C: On the Tag field, select Special Handling from the choice list
options.D: On the Special Handling field, check the box

explanation: Tags are added via the More Options (...) icon > Add Tag on a record.
wrongAnswerNotes: The regular Context Menu doesn't host Add Tag — that specific action lives under the More Options (...) icon.

text: As administrator, what must you do to access features of High Security Settings?

options.C: Add security_admin role to your user account
options.D: Use System Administration > Elevate Roles module

explanation: An admin activates elevated privileges via the User menu's Elevate Roles option to access High Security Settings.
wrongAnswerNotes: Having the security_admin role is a prerequisite, but the actual action to ACCESS those features in-session is using Elevate Roles.

text: Which one of the following statements applies to a set of fields when they are coalesced during an import?

options.A: If a match is found using the coalesce fields, the existing record is updated with the information being imported
options.B: If a match is not found using the coalesce fields, the system does not create a Transform Map
options.C: If a match is found using the coalesce fields, the system creates a new record
options.D: If a match is not found using the coalesce fields, the existing record is updated with the information being imported

explanation: If a coalesce field match is found during import, the existing record is updated with the imported data.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'creates a new record' option is backwards — a coalesce MATCH means the system updates the EXISTING record, not create a new one.

text: Your company is giving all first line workers a special T-shirt as a recognition for their hard work. Management team wants a way for employees to order the T- shirt, with the ability to specify the preferred size and color. How would you ensure that only first line workers (non-managers) can submit the order?

options.A: Create Record Producer and use the Available For list to specify First Line [sn_first_line] role
options.B: Create Catalog Item and use the Not Available list to specify the Manager Group
options.C: Create Catalog Item and use the Available For list to specify ITIL [itil] role
options.D: Create Order Guide and use the User Criteria list to specify First Line [sn_first_line] role

explanation: Create one Catalog Item and use the Not Available list to exclude the Manager Group, restricting orders to non-managers.
wrongAnswerNotes: Using the itil role would let ANY itil user order it (including managers) — the actual fix is excluding the Manager Group via the Not Available list.

text: A department manager asks an analyst to build some reports. Where do you recommend the analyst start?
explanation: Reports > Create New is the recommended starting point for building a new report.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Getting Started' sounds like a natural first stop, but the actual recommended starting module is Reports > Create New.

text: Which feature allows you to automate business logic for a particular application or process such as approvals, tasks notifications, and record operations?
explanation: Flows automate business logic such as approvals, tasks, notifications, and record operations.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Action Sequences' sounds descriptive of chained automation but isn't a real ServiceNow feature name — that capability is simply called Flows.

text: When managing tags, you can adjust who is able to see it. What are the visibility options? (Choose three.)
explanation: Tag visibility options are Groups and Users, Me, and Everyone.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Roles and Permissions' sounds like a plausible visibility option, but Tag visibility is actually controlled via Groups/Users, Me, or Everyone.

text: Which section of the ServiceNow UI allows you to perform a global search?
explanation: The Banner frame contains the global search section of the UI.
wrongAnswerNotes: The Application Navigator has its own filter for apps/modules, but the actual GLOBAL search lives in the Banner frame.

text: What type of field allows you to look up values from one other table?
explanation: A Reference field looks up values from another table.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Lookup' sounds like a natural name but isn't the real field type — looking up values from another table uses a Reference field.

text: What is used frequently to move customizations from one instance to another?
explanation: Update Sets are used frequently to move customizations from one instance to another.
wrongAnswerNotes: Options like 'Code Sets' and 'Update Packs' are invented-sounding decoys — the real, well-known mechanism is Update Sets.

text: Which tool should be used to populate commonly used fields in a form?
explanation: A Template populates commonly used fields on a form.
wrongAnswerNotes: A Reference Qualifier restricts which records show up in a reference field — it doesn't pre-populate values the way a Template does.

text: What role enables someone to authorize a request, with no other permissions on the platform?
explanation: The Approver [approver_user] role authorizes requests with no other platform permissions.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Approval Group' sounds close but isn't the real role name — the specific no-other-permissions role is Approver [approver_user].

text: What component causes a flow to run after a record has been created or updated?
explanation: A Record-based trigger runs a flow after a record is created or updated.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'On-change trigger' sounds like a plausible match, but the actual ServiceNow term for this is a Record-based trigger.

text: When a custom table is created, which access control rules are automatically created? (Choose four.)
explanation: Custom tables automatically get create, read, write, and delete ACLs.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'update' sounds like the natural fourth operation, but ServiceNow's actual ACL vocabulary uses 'write' — the four auto-created ACLs are create, read, write, delete.

text: How is the Event Log different from the Event Registry?

options.A: Event Log contains generated Events, the Event Registry is a table of Event definitions
options.B: Event Log is formatted in the Log style, the Event Registry displays different fields
options.C: Event Log lists Events that were triggered by integrations, the Event Registry lists the Events that were triggered during the day (24-hour period)
options.D: Event Log is the same as the Event Registry

explanation: The Event Log holds generated Events; the Event Registry is the table of Event definitions.
wrongAnswerNotes: The option inventing a time-based/integration-based distinction isn't real — the actual difference is Event Log (generated events) vs Event Registry (event type definitions).

text: What type of query allows you to filter list data using normal words, instead of the condition builder?
explanation: Natural Language Query lets you filter list data using plain words instead of the condition builder.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Predictive Intelligence Query' sounds plausible since Predictive Intelligence is a real feature elsewhere, but the plain-language list filter feature is specifically Natural Language Query.

text: What Service Catalog feature do you use to organize items into logical groups?
explanation: Categories organize Service Catalog items into logical groups.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Sections' sounds organizational but isn't the real Service Catalog grouping feature — that's Categories.

text: What is the most common role that has access to almost all platform features, functions, and data?
explanation: System Administrator [admin] is the most common role with near-full platform access.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Sys Admin [sys_admin]' is a near-identical sounding decoy — the real, actual role name/ID is System Administrator [admin].

text: What feature do you use to specify which users are able to access a Service Catalog Item?
explanation: User Criteria specifies which users can access a Service Catalog Item.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Can Read Role' sounds like a plausible dedicated field, but catalog item access is actually controlled through User Criteria.

text: What are the three key tables in an enterprise CMDB? (Choose three.)
explanation: The three key CMDB tables are cmdb, cmdb_ci, and cmdb_rel_ci.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'sn_cmdb' and similar 'sn_'-prefixed options are invented variants — the three real core tables are cmdb, cmdb_ci, and cmdb_rel_ci (no sn_ prefix).

text: Which ServiceNow capability allows you to provide knowledge articles, via a conversational messaging interface?
explanation: Virtual Agent provides knowledge articles through a conversational messaging interface.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Agent Assist' sounds plausible given the 'Agent' naming pattern, but the conversational KB-delivery capability is Virtual Agent.

text: What component of the ServiceNow infrastructure defines every table and field in the system?
explanation: The Dictionary defines every table and field in the system.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Schema' sounds like the obvious answer conceptually, but ServiceNow's specific component defining every table/field is the Dictionary.

text: When creating a new notification, what must you define? (Choose three.)

options.B: Settings for handing inactive user accounts
options.C: Under what conditions is the notification sent
options.E: What the content of the notification

explanation: Creating a notification requires defining the send condition, the recipients, and the content.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Associated knowledge base' isn't part of notification setup — the three real required pieces are conditions, recipients, and content.

text: Which type of ServiceNow script runs on the web browser?
explanation: A Client script runs in the web browser.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Database script' and 'Local script' aren't real ServiceNow script types — browser-side scripts are specifically Client Scripts.

text: When importing data, what happens to imported rows, if no coalesce field is specified?

options.A: All rows are rejected from the import, as coalesce field is required.
options.C: Duplicate rows are rejected from the import.
options.D: All rows are treated as new records, but errors will be flagged in the import log.

explanation: Without a coalesce field, imported rows are all treated as new records.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'all rows rejected' option assumes coalesce is mandatory — in reality, rows are simply all treated as new records without it.

text: Your customer requires that they be able to monitor which users are performing impersonations in their instance. What would you do to meet that requirement?

options.A: Add the role Log Write [sn_log_write] to the Impersonator Group
options.B: Create user update set for impersonation tracking
options.D: From User icon, select Elevate Roles
options.E: On the Impersonator role record, right click and select Create Log

explanation: Activating the glide.sys.log_impersonation property lets you monitor who performs impersonations.
wrongAnswerNotes: Adding a role doesn't enable logging — the actual mechanism is a system property, glide.sys.log_impersonation, not a role/group change.

text: How is the ServiceNow platform set up so that Administrators can easily configure their instances to send an email at the end of an upgrade?

options.A: Administrators can update the email notification named "System Upgraded" in the System Logs module by adding the appropriate User to receive it.
options.B: Administrators can update the email notification named "System Upgraded" in the Notifications module by adding the appropriate User to receive it.
options.C: Administrators can write a Client Script to send out an email to the Administrator when an Update is complete.
options.D: Administrators can write a Business Rule to send out an email to the Administrator when an Update is complete.

explanation: Update the 'System Upgraded' notification in the Notifications module to add the recipient.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'System Logs module' option is a near-identical decoy — the real module to edit this notification is the Notifications module.

text: Which tool graphically displays an infrastructure view for a configuration item (CI) and its relationship with other CIs?
explanation: Dependency View graphically shows a CI's infrastructure and its relationships to other CIs.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Dependency Map' is a very close-sounding decoy — the actual ServiceNow feature name is Dependency View.

text: You are looking at a list of Active Incidents. You want to exclude Incidents with the state of Resolved. How might you do that?

options.A: On Search, select State, type not Resolved, press enter
options.B: On State column title, right-click, select Filter Out > Resolved
options.C: On the list of records, locate and right-click on the Resolved value, select Filter Out
options.D: On the list of records, locate and right-click on the Resolved value, select Exclude
options.E: Click Funnel icon, click AND, Select Resolved, is Not, State, click Run

explanation: Right-clicking the Resolved value in a list and selecting Filter Out excludes those records.
wrongAnswerNotes: The condition-builder option describes a technically workable but convoluted approach — the quick, standard gesture is simply right-clicking the value and choosing Filter Out.

text: Which field (or fields) is used as a unique key during imports?
explanation: Coalesce Fields act as the unique key used to match records during import.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Key Fields' is the generic database term you might expect, but ServiceNow's specific import terminology is Coalesce Fields.

text: What capability allows users to create dashboards with widgets to visualize data over time in order to identify areas of improvement?
explanation: Performance Analytics builds dashboards with widgets to visualize data trends over time.
wrongAnswerNotes: Plain Reporting shows point-in-time data — the specific capability for trend dashboards over time is Performance Analytics.

text: The customer has asked that you change the default layout of the Task list. They would like these columns, in this order: • Number • Task Type • Parent • Short Description • Assignment Group • Assignee • Updated After navigating to the list, where would you click, to meet this requirement?

options.A: Click List Context Menu > Personalize List
options.B: Click List Context Menu > Configure > Columns
options.C: Right click List Gear icon > Configure > Columns
options.D: Right click on any column header, Context Menu > Configure > List Layout

explanation: Right-click any column header > Context Menu > Configure > List Layout changes and reorders list columns.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Personalize List' sounds like the simpler obvious pick, but the actual path to reorder/configure columns is via column header > Configure > List Layout.

text: Which is the base table of the configuration management database hierarchy?
explanation: cmdb_ci is the base table of the CMDB hierarchy.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'cmdb' sounds like the obvious base table by name, but it's actually just a category/grouping table — the real base of the CI hierarchy is cmdb_ci.

text: What are advantages of using Flow Designer? (Choose three.)
explanation: Flow Designer reduces technical debt, requires less manual scripting, and integrates smoothly with 3rd-party systems.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Supports advanced developers' and 'Enables complicated scripting' describe the opposite of Flow Designer's actual selling point — it's built to reduce scripting and support LESS technical builders.

text: On a filter condition, which component is always a choice list?
explanation: The Operator in a filter condition is always a choice list.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Match Criteria' isn't a standard filter builder term — the component that's always a fixed choice list (is, is not, contains, etc.) is the Operator.

text: Which framework can automatically populate values for the Priority and Category fields based on the Short description field value?
explanation: Predictive Intelligence can auto-populate Priority/Category based on Short Description.
wrongAnswerNotes: Assignment Rules route records to groups based on conditions — they don't analyze free text to auto-fill Priority/Category the way Predictive Intelligence does.

text: A customer wants to be able to identify and track components of their infrastructure that support their eCommerce service. What ServiceNow products could support this requirement? (Choose three.)
explanation: Discovery, CMDB, and Service Mapping support tracking eCommerce infrastructure components.
wrongAnswerNotes: Asset Management and Performance Analytics are real products but track different things — the three that directly identify/track infrastructure are Discovery, CMDB, and Service Mapping.

text: Which admin role is required to make changes to High Security Settings?
explanation: The security_admin role is required to change High Security Settings.
wrongAnswerNotes: Plain 'admin' doesn't have this by default — High Security Settings specifically require the separate security_admin role.

text: Security rules are defined to restrict the permissions of users from viewing and interacting with data. What are these security rules called?
explanation: Access Control Rules restrict permissions on viewing/interacting with data.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'CRUD Rules' sounds plausible since ACLs govern CRUD operations, but the actual name of these security rules is Access Control Rules.

text: What type of field has a drop down list, from which you can pick from pre-defined options?
explanation: A Choice field type presents a dropdown of pre-defined options.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Drop down' describes the visual widget, not the actual ServiceNow field TYPE name, which is Choice.

text: A user is complaining that they are seeing a blank page, when they click Create New, from your custom Inventory application. You have confirmed that they can see the Inventory application, and the Create New module on the application navigator. What could be the cause of this issue?

options.A: Create New module has a broken link
options.C: User should be using Chrome instead of Explorer for their browser
options.D: User has read role, but not the write role on the Inventory table

explanation: The user has read but not write role on the Inventory table, causing a blank Create New page.
wrongAnswerNotes: A broken link would usually show an error, not a blank page — a blank Create New form is the classic symptom of missing write role/ACL access.

text: On what part of the ServiceNow instance, would you find the option to Impersonate User?
explanation: The Impersonate User option is found in the User Menu.
wrongAnswerNotes: The Application Navigator is for finding apps/modules — Impersonate User specifically lives in the User Menu (top-right).

text: Which system property is added and set to true in order to see impersonation events in the System Log?
explanation: glide.sys.log_impersonation is set true to log impersonation events to the System Log.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'glide.sys.all_impersonation' is a plausible-sounding but incorrect property name — the actual property is glide.sys.log_impersonation.

text: What are benefits of assigning work tasks to a group, rather than to an individual? (Choose four.)

options.A: Group members can choose their tasks from My Groups Work
options.B: Groups can assign tasks to users based on on-call schedules
options.C: Site support members can pick tasks, based on Location
options.D: Groups can assign tasks to users based on skills
options.E: Group members can avoid tasks, which are nearing SLA breach
options.F: Groups can assign tasks to users based on availability

explanation: Assigning tasks to a group lets members pick tasks, and enables assignment by on-call schedule, skills, or availability.
wrongAnswerNotes: Location-based picking and avoiding SLA-nearing tasks sound plausible but aren't the documented benefits — the real four relate to on-call, skills, and availability-based assignment plus self-selection from My Groups Work.

text: Tables may have a One to Many relationships. From the Service Catalog, what are examples of tables having a one to many relationships? (Choose three.)

options.A: One Approval can have many Requests
options.B: One Request can have many Requested Items
options.C: One Requested Item can have many Approvals
options.D: One Requested Item can have many Catalog Tasks
options.E: One Cart can have many Requests

explanation: One Request has many Requested Items; one Requested Item has many Approvals and many Catalog Tasks.
wrongAnswerNotes: The Approval-to-Requests option reverses the real direction — one Requested Item can have many Approvals, not the other way around.

text: While showing a customer their Incident form, they ask to change the Priority field title to display their internal terminology PValue. How would you do that? (Choose two.)

options.A: Right click on Priority and select Configure Label
options.B: Right click on Priority and select Configure Dictionary
options.C: Right click on Priority and select Configure Display Settings
options.D: Right click on Priority and select Configure Column

explanation: Right-clicking Priority and selecting Configure Label (or editing the dictionary entry) changes the field's display title.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Configure Column' sounds plausible for changing a display title but isn't a real menu option here — the two real routes are Configure Label or editing the Dictionary entry directly.

text: What process allows users to create, categorize, review, approve and browse important information in a centralized location that is shared by the entire organization?
explanation: Knowledge Management lets users create, categorize, review, approve, and browse shared information.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Knowledge-Centered Management' sounds like an official-sounding variant, but the actual ServiceNow/ITSM process name is simply Knowledge Management.

text: How would you define an Access Control, to allow a user with itil role to have permission to create incident records?

options.A: Name: incident.None; Operation: create; Role: itil
options.B: Name: incident.Any; Operation: write; Permission: itil
options.C: Name: incident:*; Permission: write; Role: itil
options.D: Name: incident.None; Permission: create; Role: itil

explanation: The ACL uses Name: incident.None, Operation: create, Role: itil — 'Operation' is the correct ACL field name.
wrongAnswerNotes: The options using 'Permission:' instead of 'Operation:' are a subtle but important field-name mix-up to watch for.

text: What are the main components of the Form Design interface? (Choose three.)
explanation: Form Design includes Field Layout, Field Navigator, and Form Layout components.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Page Header' sounds like a plausible generic UI component but isn't one of Form Design's three real components — those are Field Layout, Field Navigator, and Form Layout.

text: What ServiceNow feature can be triggered by events, and is used to inform users about activities or updates in ServiceNow?
explanation: Notifications are triggered by events to inform users of activities/updates.
wrongAnswerNotes: Events are the TRIGGER, not the thing that informs the user — the actual user-facing feature is the Notification itself.

text: What are examples of Core tables in the ServiceNow platform?
explanation: User, Task, and Incident are examples of Core tables.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'Configuration, Connect, Chat' option strings together real ServiceNow-sounding words that aren't actually 'Core tables' — the genuine core table examples are User, Task, and Incident.

text: When you set a policy that is applied to all data entered into the platform (UI, Import Sets, or Web Services), where does this policy run by default?
explanation: A Data Policy (applied to all entry methods) runs on the Server by default.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Client' is intuitive since policies affect what users see, but a Data Policy (covering all entry channels) specifically runs on the Server.

text: On a Business Rule, the When setting determines at what point the rule executes. What are the options for specifying that timing?
explanation: Business Rule timing options are Before, After, Async, and Display.
wrongAnswerNotes: Insert/Update/Delete/Query are real Business Rule settings too, but they're the triggering ACTIONS, not the TIMING — the timing options are Before, After, Async, Display.

text: Which feature helps to automatically allocate a critical, high-priority, service request to the appropriate assignment group or team member?
explanation: Assignment Rules auto-allocate high-priority requests to the appropriate group/member.
wrongAnswerNotes: Predictive Intelligence can suggest values but the specific feature that automatically ROUTES requests to a group/member is an Assignment Rule.

text: You have been asked to configure a form so an employee could order tablet and select the standard accessory options to purchase with it. These standard options are: carrying case, screen cleaner, tablet stand, and screen protector. What approach would you take? (Choose three.)

options.A: Create Catalog Item for the Tablet, and add a variable set to the form, for the accessory options.
options.B: Create a Record producer, and on the form, add a check box variable for each accessory option.
options.C: On Shopping Cart configuration, select option lo show the Add Accessories button.
options.D: Create one Catalog item for each: tablet, carrying case, screen cleaner, tablet stand, and screen protector.
options.E: Create Catalog Item for the tablet, and on the form, add a check box variable for each accessory option.

explanation: Approach: add a variable set for accessories, enable the cart's Add Accessories option, or add checkbox variables directly on the catalog item.
wrongAnswerNotes: Creating five separate catalog items technically works but is the wrong/impractical approach — bundling via variable sets or checkbox variables on ONE item is the intended method. (Flagged low-confidence — verify exact best practice.)

text: An order for new office equipment has been placed through the Service Catalog. How would you view the lists of requests after the orders have been placed?

options.B: In the Navigation Filter, type “requests.list" and press the Enter key
options.D: All > Service Catalog > Open Records > Items

explanation: All > Service Catalog > Requests shows the list of requests placed.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Open Records > Items' sounds like a plausible similarly-named path, but the real module for viewing placed requests is All > Service Catalog > Requests.

text: Here is an example of the criteria set for a knowledge base: • Companies: ACME North America • Departments: HR • Groups: ACME Managers • Match All: Yes In this example, what users would have access to this knowledge base?

options.A: Members of the ACME Manager group, who are also members of HR Department and part of ACME North America
options.B: Employees of ACME North America, who are members of HR Department or the ACME Managers group
options.C: Users which are members of either ACME North America, or HR Department, or ACME Managers group
options.D: Members of the ACME Managers group, and HR department, regardless of geography

explanation: With Match All = Yes, access requires membership in all three criteria: ACME Managers group AND HR Department AND ACME North America.
wrongAnswerNotes: The OR-logic option swaps in OR — but 'Match All: Yes' specifically means AND across all three criteria (company AND department AND group), not any one of them.

text: When using Flow Designer, what is the Flow Execution initiated by?
explanation: A trigger initiates Flow Execution.
wrongAnswerNotes: A subflow can be called FROM a flow, but what actually INITIATES flow execution in the first place is the trigger.

text: Which features ensures data consistency while importing data using import sets and web services?
explanation: Data Policy ensures data consistency for import sets and web services.
wrongAnswerNotes: UI Policy only applies to form interactions — for import sets and web services specifically, it's Data Policy that ensures consistency.

text: Which data consistency settings can be achieved using UI Policy? (Choose three.)

options.A: Setting fields to accept the data in an expected format
options.B: Setting fields to accept the data with ‘n’ number of characters

explanation: UI Policy can set fields hidden, read-only, or mandatory.
wrongAnswerNotes: Format validation and character-length limits are actually Dictionary/field attributes, not things a UI Policy sets — UI Policy specifically controls hidden, read-only, and mandatory.

text: How would you navigate to the Schema map for a table?

options.A: System Definition > Tables; Select Table; Go to Related links and click Show Schema Map
options.B: System Dictionary > Show Schema Map; Select Table
options.C: System Definition > Show Schema Map; Select Table
options.D: System Definition > Dictionary; Select Table; Go to Related links and click Show Schema Map

explanation: System Definition > Tables; select the table; Related Links > Show Schema Map.
wrongAnswerNotes: Starting from Dictionary sounds equally plausible, but the documented path specifically starts at System Definition > Tables.

text: The Report Designer contains different sections for configuring your report. Which section is used to specify the name of the report, and the table or data source for the report?
explanation: The Data section of Report Designer is where you choose the table/data source for the report.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Properties' sounds like the natural place to name a report, but in Report Designer that's actually handled within the Data section where you also pick the source table.

text: You are asked to create an option in the Service Catalog, which will allow a user to click Get Help and describe the issue they are having. These forms should create incident records, which are automatically routed to the Service Desk. Which method would you use?
explanation: A Record Producer creates incident records automatically routed to the Service Desk.
wrongAnswerNotes: A plain Catalog Item doesn't generate task records automatically — for auto-creating and routing incident records, you need a Record Producer.

text: What action will allow you to personalize layouts of columns in a list?

options.B: Click Gear Icon > Personalize window options > Select the appropriate columns
options.C: Select the column to be personalized and right at the header > Choose the options to personalize
options.D: Select the column to be personalized > Click Edit icon (Pencil) > Choose the options to personalize

explanation: Right-clicking the column header lets you choose options to personalize the list layout.
wrongAnswerNotes: The Gear icon path sounds plausible but isn't how column personalization works — you right-click the column header itself.

text: What is the result of the order in which access controls are evaluated?

options.A: Ensures user has access to the fields in a table, before considering their access to the table
options.B: Ensures user can get to work as quickly as possible
options.C: Ensures user has access to the application, before evaluating access to a module within the application
options.D: Ensures user has access to a table, before evaluating access to a field in the table

explanation: Access control evaluation order ensures table-level access is confirmed before field-level access.
wrongAnswerNotes: The 'field access before table access' option reverses the real order — table-level access is always confirmed BEFORE field-level access is evaluated, not after.

text: What are the components that make up a filter condition? (Choose three.)
explanation: A filter condition is made up of a Field, Operator, and Value.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Match Criteria' and 'Column' sound plausible, but the three official components of a filter condition are Field, Operator, and Value.

text: Which component of a table contains a piece of data for one record?
explanation: A Field is the table component holding one piece of data for a record.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Element' is a plausible generic term, but ServiceNow's specific name for one piece of record data is a Field.

text: If a knowledge base has no access details specified, what users are able to read articles in that knowledge base?

options.B: Any user with an article's permalink

explanation: If no access details are specified, any active user can read the knowledge base's articles.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'No users' sounds like the safer assumption, but ServiceNow's actual default (with no access details specified) opens it up to any active user.

text: What attributes can you manage, using System Properties > Basic Configuration UI16? (Choose five.)
explanation: Basic Configuration UI16 manages items like browser tab title, module text color, base theme, header background color, and banner image.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Preferred browser' and 'Animation style' sound like plausible customization options but aren't part of Basic Configuration UI16 — that module covers branding/theme items like tab title, colors, and banner image. (Flagged low-confidence — verify the exact five.)

text: What are examples of UI Actions relating to forms? (Choose three.)
explanation: Form-related UI Actions include Form Buttons, Form Context Menu, and Form Links.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Form Columns' sounds parallel to genuine options but isn't a real UI Action category for forms — the three real ones are Form Buttons, Form Context Menu, and Form Links.

text: A customer wants to use a client script to validate things on a form in order to make sure the submission makes sense. What type of client script would you recommend to meet this requirement?
explanation: onSubmit() client scripts validate form data before submission.
wrongAnswerNotes: onLoad() validates/reacts when the form first loads, not at submission — validating the submission specifically calls for onSubmit().

text: The wait time for end users is based on the round-trip between the client and the server. What activities are included in the round-trips?
explanation: The client-server round trip consists of a Request and a Response.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Save+Update' and 'Write+Read' sound plausible as generic pairs, but the actual client-server round-trip terminology is simply Request and Response.

text: You are editing a new incident record and would like the "Save" button to be located on the Form header. Which action would need to be taken for that button to appear?

options.A: Context Menu > Form Design > add the “Save” button.
options.B: All > System Properties > UI Properties > Turn on the “glide.ui.advanced” property.
options.C: All > System Properties > UI Properties > Turn on the “Save” button.
options.D: Context Meru > Form Layout > add the “Save” button.

explanation: Enabling the glide.ui.advanced property surfaces the Save button in the form header.
wrongAnswerNotes: Adding it via Form Design sounds like the direct approach, but surfacing the header Save button is actually controlled by the glide.ui.advanced system property. (Flagged low-confidence — verify this specific property.)

text: What do you click when you have made modifications to your report, and you want to see the results without saving?
explanation: Clicking Run previews report results without saving changes.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Preview' sounds like the intuitive term for this action, but the actual button used to test report results without saving is Run.

text: A colleague wants to rearrange the columns on their My Work List. Once the user has navigated to the list, where should they navigate to select and arrange the columns

options.A: Right click on any column header, Context Menu > Configure > List Layout
options.B: Click List Context Menu > Configure > List Layout
options.C: Click List Context Menu > Personalize List

explanation: Right-click any column header > Context Menu > Configure > List Layout rearranges My Work List columns.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Personalize List' sounds like a simpler alternate route, but the documented path is right-clicking the column header > Configure > List Layout.

text: When a flow runs an action, it generates a runtime value, which stays the same for the duration of the flow. What is the name of this runtime value?
explanation: The Trigger runtime value (based on the triggering record) stays constant throughout the flow's execution.
wrongAnswerNotes: Data pill is how you REFERENCE values generally, but the specific runtime value that stays constant for the whole flow is the Trigger runtime value.

text: What is the name of the string that displays filter criteria?
explanation: The Breadcrumb displays the current filter criteria as a string.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Menu' is a generic-sounding guess, but the specific string displaying active filter criteria is called the Breadcrumb.

text: On Access Control Definitions, what are ways you can set the permissions on a Table? (Choose three.)

options.D: Script that sets the answer variable to true or false

explanation: ACL permissions on a table can be set via Conditional Expressions, Roles, and Script.
wrongAnswerNotes: CRUD describes WHAT operation is being protected, not a way to grant permission — and Groups aren't directly used on ACLs (roles are) — the three real mechanisms are Conditional Expressions, Roles, and Script.

text: Which role can manage multiple knowledge bases?
explanation: The knowledge_admin role can manage multiple knowledge bases.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'kb_admin' sounds like a very plausible abbreviated role name, but the actual role for managing multiple KBs is knowledge_admin.

text: Which statement correctly describes the differences between a Client Script and a Business Rule?

options.A: A Client Script executes before a record is loaded and a Business Rule executes after a record is loaded
options.B: A Client Script executes on the server and a Business Rule executes on the client
options.C: A Client Script executes on the client and a Business Rule executes on the server
options.D: A Client Script executes before a record is loaded and a Business Rule executes after a record is updated

explanation: A Client Script executes on the client (browser); a Business Rule executes on the server.
wrongAnswerNotes: The option stating it backwards (Client Script on server, Business Rule on client) is exactly reversed — Client Scripts run on the client, Business Rules run on the server.

text: An order from the Service Catalog has been placed. Two records in the Platform are created as a result. Which two records are associated with this newly ordered item? (Choose two.)
explanation: Placing a Service Catalog order creates an sc_req_item record and an sc_task record.
wrongAnswerNotes: An Incident record isn't created by a normal catalog order — the two real records generated are an sc_req_item and an sc_task.

text: In Flow Designer, where is the data from an action stored so it can be used in subsequent actions in the flow?
explanation: Action output data is stored in a Data Pill for use in later flow actions.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Data Element' is a plausible-sounding generic term, but ServiceNow's specific name for this stored action output is a Data Pill.

text: What is the definition of a group?

options.A: A collection of subject matter experts

explanation: A group is a collection of users.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'A department' is a natural real-world guess but too narrow — a ServiceNow group is generically just a collection of users, which can represent a department or many other things.

text: A new employee joins the IT department and needs to perform work assigned to Network and Hardware groups. How would you set up their access? (Choose three.)

options.A: Add User Account to Hardware group
options.B: Add User Account to IT Knowledgebase
options.D: Add User Account to itil group
options.E: Add User Account to Network group

explanation: Set up access by creating the user account and adding it to both the Network and Hardware groups.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'itil group' is a trap — itil is a ROLE, not a group you add someone to; the real setup is creating the account and adding it to the Network and Hardware groups.

text: The ServiceNow platform supports a wide variety of plug-and-play applications. You can choose from the included workflows of build your own workflow: Which of these workflows are included in the platform? (Choose three.)
explanation: The platform ships with Customer, Employee, and IT plug-and-play workflows.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Infrastructure Workflows' sounds like a plausible product line, but the three actual plug-and-play categories are Customer, Employee, and IT Workflows.

text: Which allows the creation of a task-based record from Service Catalog?
explanation: Record Producers create a task-based record from the Service Catalog.
wrongAnswerNotes: Flow Designer can automate what happens AFTER an order, but the thing that actually creates a task-based record from the catalog is a Record Producer.

text: Which testing framework is used to test ServiceNow Applications?
explanation: Automated Test Framework (ATF) is used to test ServiceNow applications.
wrongAnswerNotes: Selenium is a real, well-known general web-testing tool, but ServiceNow's own built-in framework is specifically the Automated Test Framework (ATF).

text: What are different types of Data Sources, which may be imported into ServiceNow? (Choose four.)
explanation: Data sources importable into ServiceNow include Local Sources, JDBC, Network Server, and LDAP connections.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'DataHub' and 'Implementation Spoke' sound like plausible modern integration terms, but the real classic data source types are Local Sources, JDBC, Network Server, and LDAP.

text: Which ServiceNow utility provides a modern interactive graphical interface to visualize configuration items and their relationships?
explanation: Dependency View gives a modern interactive graphical interface for CI relationships.
wrongAnswerNotes: Business Service Map is a real, related CMDB visualization but the one specifically described as 'modern interactive' for CI relationships is Dependency View.

text: What module do you use to access the reports that are available to you?
explanation: Reports > View/Run is the module used to access reports available to you.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Self-Service > My Reports' sounds like a plausible personal landing page, but the actual module to access available reports is Reports > View/Run.

text: A customer has asked for the following updates to a form: • Make Resolution code Mandatory, when state is changed to Resolved • Hide Major Incident check box, unless logged in user has Major Incident Manager role What type of rule(s) would you use to implement this requirement?
explanation: UI Policy handles conditional mandatory fields and role-based field visibility.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Form Constraint' sounds tailor-made for this scenario but isn't a real ServiceNow rule type — both requirements (conditional mandatory + role-based visibility) are handled by UI Policy.

text: What resource can you use to view details of the tables and configuration items (CIs) associated with a particular use case?

options.D: Common Service Data Model (CSDM) product view

explanation: The CSDM product view lets you see tables/CIs associated with a particular use case.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'CMDB Use Case Modeler' sounds like the most literal tool name for this, but the actual resource for this is the CSDM product view.

text: Which ServiceNow utility gives a Service Desk agent the ability to trace from a Service having an issue, to see which CIs supporting that service have active issues?
explanation: CI Dependency View lets an agent trace from a Service down to the CIs supporting it and see which of those CIs currently have active issues affecting that service.
wrongAnswerNotes: Event Management Homepage surfaces alerts/events but doesn't trace service-to-CI dependency; Service Dashboard and CI Health Dashboard show status summaries, not the specific dependency-tracing view this question describes.

text: An IT manager is responsible for the Network and Hardware assignment groups, each group contains 5 team members. These team members are working on many tasks, but the manager cannot see any tasks on the Service Desk > My Groups Work list. What could explain this?

options.A: The Assignment Group manager field is empty.
options.B: The manager does not have the itil role.
options.C: The manager is not a member of the Service Desk group.
options.D: The manager is not a member of the Network and Hardware groups.

explanation: 'My Groups Work' only shows tasks for groups the logged-in user is actually a member of. Being set as the group's manager doesn't automatically add someone as a member, so if the manager isn't personally in the Network and Hardware groups, their tasks won't appear on this list.
wrongAnswerNotes: An empty manager field would be unrelated to what a member sees on their own work list; the itil role controls broader ITSM access, not specifically which groups show on 'My Groups Work'; membership in the Service Desk group is irrelevant since this list is scoped to the Network and Hardware groups, not Service Desk.

text: Which tool is used to define relationships between fields in an import set table and a target table?
explanation: A Transform Map defines the field-to-field mapping between an import set (staging) table and the target table it's importing data into.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Schema Map', 'Field Transformer', and 'Transform Schema' are not real ServiceNow feature names — Transform Map is the actual term used for this mapping tool.

text: On the knowledge base record, which tab would you use to define which users are not able to write articles to the knowledge base?
explanation: The 'Cannot Author' related list on a Knowledge Base record explicitly blocks specified users or groups from contributing/writing articles to that base.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Can Contribute', 'Can Read', 'Can Write', and 'Can Author' all define who CAN take an action — only 'Cannot Author' is the explicit block list this question is asking about.

text: A customer requests the following data quality measures be added: ✑ Incident numbers should be read only, on all lists and forms, for all users. ✑ Short Description field should be mandatory, on all records, across all applications, on Insert. Which type of policy would you use to meet this requirement?
explanation: A UI Policy can make a field read-only or mandatory across all forms and lists for a table based on conditions, which covers both requirements described here.
wrongAnswerNotes: 'Data Quality Policy', 'Dictionary Design Policy', 'UI Data Policy', and 'Field Criteria Policy' are not real ServiceNow policy types; Data Policy specifically enforces rules outside the UI (e.g. via API/import) and isn't the primary tool for form/list field behavior described here.
